response: {"name":"Public CLIP Database and iCLIP data processing","description":"Processing of publicly available iCLIP, HITS-CLIP, and PAR-CLIP datasets downloaded from SRA/ERA, including adapter trimming, PCR duplicate removal, and peak calling, following a pipeline analogous to eCLIP-seq with method-specific distinctions.","data_type":"sequencing","raw_data_source":"SRA/ERA (accessions listed in Supplementary Table 1)","steps":[{"step_number":1,"description":"Raw CLIP-seq data (iCLIP, HITS-CLIP, PAR-CLIP) are downloaded from SRA/ERA as listed in Supplementary Table 1.","input_data":"Public CLIP-seq datasets (iCLIP, HITS-CLIP, PAR-CLIP) from SRA/ERA","output_data":"Raw sequencing reads (FASTQ files)","software":null,"software_version":null,"parameters":{},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":2,"description":"Adapter trimming is performed once using cutadapt, in contrast to eCLIP-seq where it may be performed multiple times.","input_data":"Raw sequencing reads (FASTQ files)","output_data":"Adapter-trimmed sequencing reads","software":"cutadapt","software_version":null,"parameters":{"trimming_rounds":"1"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":3,"description":"Trimmed reads are aligned to the genome following the eCLIP-seq pipeline; datasets with fewer than 100,000 uniquely mapped reads are discarded.","input_data":"Adapter-trimmed sequencing reads","output_data":"Aligned reads (BAM files); low-read-count datasets discarded","software":"STAR","software_version":null,"parameters":{"min_uniquely_mapped_reads":"100000"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":4,"description":"PCR duplicate removal is performed in a library-type-specific manner: for iCLIP, random-mers (UMIs) in reads are used for duplicate removal; for HITS-CLIP and PAR-CLIP, reads sharing the same start position are treated as PCR duplicates and removed.","input_data":"Aligned reads (BAM files)","output_data":"Non-duplicate aligned reads (BAM files)","software":null,"software_version":null,"parameters":{"iCLIP_method":"random-mer (UMI)-based duplicate removal","HITS-CLIP_method":"same start position deduplication","PAR-CLIP_method":"same start position deduplication"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":5,"description":"Peak calling is performed on non-duplicate reads only, following the same approach as for eCLIP experiments.","input_data":"Non-duplicate aligned reads (BAM files)","output_data":"Called peaks (BED/cluster files)","software":"CLIPper","software_version":null,"parameters":{"input_reads":"non-duplicate reads only"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":6,"description":"Usable read density plots and smoothened kernel density histograms are generated in Matlab using the distributionPlot package with default settings.","input_data":"Usable read count statistics per dataset","output_data":"Usable read density plots and kernel density histograms","software":"Matlab / distributionPlot package","software_version":null,"parameters":{"settings":"default"},"code_reference":null}],"figures_produced":["Figure 1","Figure 1a","Figure 1b–c","Figure 1b","Figure 1d","Figure 2","Figure 2a","Figure 2b","Figure 2c","Figure 2d","Figure 2e","Figure 3","Figure 3a–b","Figure 3a","Figure 3b","Figure 3c","Figure 3d","Figure 3e","Figure 3f","Figure 4","Figure 4a–d","Figure 4a","Figure 4b","Figure 4c–d"]}
